verses.text: I come to visions; I knew a man 'caught up to the third heaven.' Heavenly glimpse. He was granted an extraordinary revelation.
I come to visions; I knew a man 'caught up to the third heaven.' Heavenly glimpse. He was granted an extraordinary revelation.
He heard 'unspeakable words'; of such a one I will glory, not of myself. Reserved. He speaks of it humbly, almost as another.
He heard 'unspeakable words'; of such a one I will glory, not of myself. Reserved. He speaks of it humbly, almost as another.
He heard 'unspeakable words'; of such a one I will glory, not of myself. Reserved. He speaks of it humbly, almost as another.
Lest I be exalted, there was given 'a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan.' A humbling thorn. God allows pain to keep him low.
Lest I be exalted, there was given 'a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan.' A humbling thorn. God allows pain to keep him low.
I besought the Lord thrice; 'My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.' Sufficient grace. God's strength is best shown in our weakness.
I besought the Lord thrice; 'My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.' Sufficient grace. God's strength is best shown in our weakness.
Therefore I take pleasure in infirmities; 'when I am weak, then am I strong.' Strong in weakness. Conscious weakness draws down Christ's power.
I am a fool in glorying; 'in nothing am I behind the... apostles, though I be nothing.' Humble apostle. He owns his standing, yet counts himself nothing.
I am a fool in glorying; 'in nothing am I behind the... apostles, though I be nothing.' Humble apostle. He owns his standing, yet counts himself nothing.
I am a fool in glorying; 'in nothing am I behind the... apostles, though I be nothing.' Humble apostle. He owns his standing, yet counts himself nothing.
I will not be burdensome; 'I seek not yours, but you'; I will gladly spend and be spent. Selfless love. He wants their souls, not their goods.
I will not be burdensome; 'I seek not yours, but you'; I will gladly spend and be spent. Selfless love. He wants their souls, not their goods.
Did I make gain of you by Titus? we walked in the same spirit. No greed. His conduct was open and honest.
Did I make gain of you by Titus? we walked in the same spirit. No greed. His conduct was open and honest.
Did I make gain of you by Titus? we walked in the same spirit. No greed. His conduct was open and honest.
We do all for your edifying; I fear to find debates, envyings, and uncleanness. Pastoral fear. He dreads finding sin unrepented among them.
